summary: Iteration 2 focused on the new SLA/Insights feature. Added /app/backend/tests/test_insights.py (12 new tests). Total suite: 49/49 pytest cases PASS across auth, users, vendors, documents, analytics summary+insights, transitions log, aging, RBAC, return-rate, weekly trend, category completeness, country risk, CSV export, PWA, settings. Frontend /insights page renders end-to-end with real backend data: 4 KPIs, Dwell-Time bar chart, Return vs Approved bars, 8-week trend line, Handler Performance table (2 rows), Aging Report table (10 rows) with correct data-testids, Country Risk + Category Completeness. All pre-existing behaviours (theme, admin CRUD, vendor CRUD, documents, audit log) unchanged.

issues: Benign Recharts 'width(-1) height(-1)' console warnings during first mount before ResponsiveContainer measures — same as iteration 1, not a real bug.

critical_code_review_comments: analytics_insights() re-reads all vendors and transitions into memory on every call (up to 2000 vendors, 20000 transitions). Fine for current scale but should be moved to a mongo aggregation pipeline or cached (30s TTL matches the frontend refetchInterval) before scaling past a few thousand vendors.

sla_by_handler credits the *last handler that touched the vendor* — if a reviewer submits and a different approver later approves, the approver gets credit for the full DRAFT→APPROVED elapsed time. Consider crediting the approver only for their leg, or splitting per handler.

DRAFT dwell time uses vendor.created_at as start, but if a vendor is later moved back into DRAFT (not currently possible via workflow but not prevented) the dwell math becomes negative and is silently dropped (`days >= 0` guard). Fine, but worth an explicit invariant.

status_since is set on every transition but never seeded on legacy vendors created before this change — aging falls back to updated_at / created_at (already handled). Consider a one-time migration to backfill status_since = updated_at for older records.
server.py is now ~825 lines (was ~640). Refactoring into routers/{auth, users, vendors, documents, analytics}.py is now overdue.
The Insights JSX file mixes chart rendering, KPI computation and table rendering in one 350-line component. Extracting each section into its own file would improve maintainability.
seed_data_creation: Insights tests created ~5 fresh TEST_-prefixed vendors (TEST InsightsFlow, TEST InsRBAC, TEST InsReturn, TEST InsAging, TEST DeleteMe) and 1 TEST_ins_rev_* reviewer user. These are exercised through the full status workflow and left in place (harmless, prefixed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full backend regression is in /app/backend/tests/{test_backend.py,test_insights.py}. Run with `pytest -n 2 backend/tests --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. The Insights UI is data-driven from GET /api/analytics/insights (30s refetchInterval). Key data-testids: insights-page, handler-row-<user_id>, aging-row-<vendor_id>, nav-insights. Insights page has no filter controls yet — all data is unconditional. Category completeness is sorted ascending by rate (lowest coverage first). sla_by_handler is sorted by approved_count desc.
rca of the issue: No issues to root-cause. One test authored initially (avg_days_to_approved > 0) had a precision problem because sub-second elapsed times round to 0.00 days at 2 decimals — relaxed to >= 0 with an inline comment. Not a product bug.
